name: always_unfold_intensional_bridge
reason: always_unfold is external_body bridging always's TempPred::new closure-wrapped forall to a bare forall — resolves closure identity gap without proof

name: implies_apply_closure_bridge
reason: implies_apply is external_body bridging p.implies(q)'s TempPred::new closure-wrapped implication to modus ponens — resolves closure identity gap without proof

name: eventually_proved_by_witness_closure_bridge
reason: eventually_proved_by_witness is external_body bridging a concrete witness to eventually's TempPred::new closure-wrapped existential — resolves closure identity gap without proof

name: always_p_or_eventually_q_induction_bridge
reason: always_p_or_eventually_q is external_body performing temporal induction — derives always(p => □p ∨ ◇q) from a step condition, bridging multiple layers of closure nesting (always/implies/or/later/eventually) without proof

name: transform_leads_to_with_until_bridge
code:
proof fn phi_5_transform_leads_to_with_until_bridge(spec: TempPred<int>, next: TempPred<int>, p1: TempPred<int>, q1: TempPred<int>, p2: TempPred<int>, q2: TempPred<int>, ex: Execution<int>)
    requires
        spec.entails(p1.leads_to(q1)),
        spec.entails(always(p2.and(next).implies(later(p2).or(later(q2))))),
        spec.entails(always(next)),
        spec.satisfied_by(ex),
    ensures
        p1.and(p2).leads_to((q1.and(p2)).or(q2)).satisfied_by(ex),
{
    transform_leads_to_with_until::<int>(spec, next, p1, q1, p2, q2);
}
reason: chains all four external_body axioms to derive that p1∧p2 leads to (q1∧p2)∨q2 — if any intensional bridge is unsound, this enables combining leads-to with persistence/until reasoning unsoundly
